summary: Gruber provides a business update on Daring Fireball's monetization efforts, covering three areas: T-shirt sales and memberships, sponsorships, and a Bloggies nomination. He frames his goal as becoming a successful full-time independent Mac writer, analogous to independent Mac developers. Membership revenue has exceeded expectations but isn't yet a full salary, and he's adding perks like a members-only mailing list to attract more supporters. The sponsorship program, featuring tasteful text-based ads from Mac-ecosystem companies, is also growing. He emphasizes that the core content remains free and memberships are a way to support that work.
key_points: Gruber is attempting to make independent Mac writing a viable full-time career, modeled on the success of independent Mac developers
The membership model provides perks (RSS feeds, mailing list access, T-shirts) as token rewards, but the real value proposition is supporting free content
His advertising philosophy insists on small, text-based, non-animated ads with a maximum of one per page and only five sponsor slots total
Membership revenue has exceeded expectations but still falls short of replacing a salary
He's adding a members-only mailing list partly to improve signal-to-noise ratio and partly to incentivize fence-sitters to join
money_quotes: If there are successful full-time independent Mac developers, why not successful full-time independent Mac writers, too?
The more readers who support this site by becoming members, the more time I'll be able to spend writing.
The intention isn't that these members-only perquisites are, in and of themselves, worth the price of a membership. What's truly valuable here is the content, and that is free for everyone.
Becoming a member is a way for you to support this free content, and the members-only features are token rewards for doing so.
By keeping ads relatively small, text-based, and non-animated, they're noticeable without being distracting.
I will only accept sponsorships for products or services that I believe will be of interest to Daring Fireball readers, and which are worth their attention.
key_insight: Gruber articulates an early blueprint for sustainable independent web publishing built on reader memberships and tasteful, limited sponsorships — a model that would prove prescient for the creator economy.
